page: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-32 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this server features 32 vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with simultaneous multithreading enabled at two threads per core. It is configured with 128 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4 GB of RAM per vCPU. The CPU includes 32 KB of L1 data cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities. In stress-ng single-core CPU testing, the instance scores in the top 25% with a result of 2696.38. Multi-core performance, measured by stress-ng at 43202.89, falls within the average range. Memory bandwidth tests show average performance, with read-only small block cached speeds at 2037.17 GB/sec and uncached large block write speeds at 52.3 GB/sec. Large-model LLM inference is a notable bottleneck, with prompt processing for a 70B model scoring in the bottom 25% at 6.55 tokens/sec. However, text generation for a small 135M model is strong at 257.15 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the n2d-standard-32 offers a balanced resource density suitable for mainstream enterprise workloads without the premium cost of specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. The lack of local NVMe storage means storage-heavy workloads must rely on network-attached persistent disks, which may introduce latency tradeoffs. This instance is best suited for general-purpose workloads, including static web serving, database operations, software compilation, and running small-to-medium scale machine learning inference tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-32 is a general-purpose virtual machine configured with 32 vCPUs and 128 GB of RAM, running on an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with x86_64 architecture. It features 16 physical cores with dedicated CPU allocation and 4 GB of memory per vCPU, but lacks bundled local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark data shows strong single-core CPU performance and efficient text generation for small LLM models, while multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform in the average tier. Conversely, large-model LLM inference represents a performance tradeoff, scoring in the bottom 25%. Given its balanced resource allocation and qualitative cost-efficiency profile, the n2d-standard-32 is well-suited for standard enterprise workloads, including static web serving, software compilation, database management, and moderate-scale application hosting.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-32 is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor, delivering balanced performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-32 features 32 vCPUs and 128 GB RAM on an AMD EPYC processor, optimized for general-purpose enterprise workloads.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and a standard one-to-four vCPU-to-RAM ratio for general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores.
Equipped with 128 GB of system memory providing 4 GB of RAM per vCPU.
Delivers strong single-core CPU performance and small-model LLM text generation.
Shows average performance in multi-core benchmarks, memory bandwidth, and database operations.
Best suited for general-purpose applications, web serving, and software compilation.
